item.title: Noel Burton-Krahn
item.description: Noel Burton-Krahn. I build software (backend, data, and graphics-y experiments) and keep notes here.

item.title: Calculating π with Archimedes' Method of Exhaustion
item.description: `0=p2`1`i+1=p2r1¡q1¡`i2411r=1ab TikZ source Copy to clipboard \begin{document} % Polygon colour palette (adjust here) \definecolor{polyA}{RGB}{20,60,180} % square: deep blue \definecolor{polyB}{RGB}{30,120,120} % octagon: blue/green blend \definecolor{polyC}{RGB}{20,160,80} % 16-gon: greenish \colo...

item.description: This page embeds the interactive demo.

item.title: Data Science Meetup: Delirium Detection With CNNs
item.description: Introduction Noel Burton-Krahn Delirium Detection in EEGs Got baseStudyEDF: control and delerium EEGs Total Control: 27 Total Delirium: 25 Neural Networks Images from CS231n Convolutional Neural Networks for Visual Recognition Convolutional Networks Deep Neural Networks Demo Digit Classifier 2D Visu...

item.title: Communicating with vegtative patients
item.description: Imagine if you were completely conscious but couldn’t move or speak? Neuroscientist Adrian Owen has been using brain-imaging techniques to detect consciousness in patients who are presumed to be vegetative, sometimes for many years.

item.title: Deep Learning for Medicine
item.description: Machine learning has made great strides in the last few years, and Convolutional Neural Networks have demonstrated some very impressive results in image and speech processing.

item.title: Battlesnakes
item.description: Genetic programming for the win! This is my entry into the 2017 Battlesnake competition. See them in action! Git repo.

item.title: Projects and Resume
item.description: Noel Burton-Krahn — code samples Here are some samples of my own code from favourite past projects.

item.title: If a number is divisible by 3, then so is the sum of its digits
item.description: A toy proof of a math trick I learned in elementary school.

item.title: Emacs Advanced Features Tutorial
item.description: The good bits of Emacs, after the online tutorial without too much kool-aid
